summary: Add initial voice governed state encoding two high-signal session behaviors from Xavier's stated collaboration preferences.
rationale: Two behavioral patterns have been consistently observed in session context and explicitly stated by Xavier: (1) never assume git/fork fluency — always surface branch/remote context before git advice; (2) flag disagreement at most once per decision point, then follow owner direction unless blocked by policy or secrets. These are low-controversy, high-leverage rules that reduce session re-derivation cost. Promoting them to governed state makes them durable across all Claude sessions.
proposed_diff_summary: Create users/cici/governed-state/voice/session-behavior.json with two entries: (a) git_context_always_restate: true, (b) disagreement_limit_per_decision: 1. Mark voice surface status as 'active' in surface-map.json once approved.
